attacking_style: Excels in technical, uphill time trial finales where bike handling and power application trump pure watts. Strongest in second half of TTs after strategic pacing, often trailing at intermediate checks before surging in technical closing kilometers.
team_tactics: Groupama-FDJ employs superior reconnaissance and pacing protocols, with teammates like Armirail setting early benchmarks to establish target splits. Team appears to prioritize technical course previews over raw power output.
terrain_preference: Technical uphill time trial finales on familiar roads are optimal (gained 11+ seconds in second half vs Sheffield, dominated uphill TdS finale). Exposed on pure power climbs when accelerations occur; unable to follow explosive attacks from pure climbers like Evenepoel despite holding race lead.
vulnerability: Cannot respond to sudden accelerations from GC climbers despite strong baseline power. Poor pacing strategy in three-week Grand Tours (faded final third at TDF 2021). Power-to-weight appears sacrificed when attempting climbing transformations.
key_patterns: Consistently trails at intermediate time checks but gains time in technical second halves (2023 TdS, 2021 TDF)
Strongest on home Swiss roads where local knowledge compounds technical skills (multiple TdS wins, TDF stage)
Unable to follow explosive accelerations from lighter climbers despite holding race lead or strong position (Evenepoel 2x)
Identifies tactical opportunities in chase scenarios, willing to attack solo when sprint uncertain (E3 podium)
Pacing strategy fails in final week of Grand Tours, fading after strong early performances
form_trajectory: negative: Lost yellow jersey inability to respond to attacks in 2023 despite earlier TT dominance. Weight loss experiments appear to compromise core TT strength without delivering climbing gains needed for GC.
